hypothesis: Incorporating protein pocket flexibility information into the pharmacophore reference set construction—by deriving consensus pharmacophore features from ensemble docking poses across multiple receptor conformations rather than a single crystal structure—will improve the biological relevance of pharmacophore-guided generative models such that generated molecules exhibit significantly higher hit rates in experimental binding assays compared to molecules generated using static single-conformation pharmacophores, even when both approaches produce comparable in silico docking scores.
